notes: On 25 July 2018, 2018: Citizens protested in Limassol, calling for the suspension of construction works on a petrol station on Dimokratias Street due to health and environmental concerns.

On 6 September 2018, 2018 in Podgorica, Montenegro the Trade Union of Administration and Judiciary of Montenegro organized a protest over suspension of Jelena Dragovic, president of the Trade Union of National Parks of Montenegro.
On 18 June 1500 people, mainly Hungarians, protested in Miercurea Ciuc, demanding autonomy for the SzekelyLand and suspension of the nationalization of the Valea Uzului war cemetery.
On 3 November 2019, tens of people protested in the Aviatiei neighborhood of Bucharest, demanding a solution to the suspension of hot water provision.

On 23 November 2019, citizens held a protest outside Parliament in Skopje against Ilovica mine concession over health concerns and demanded its suspension before the establishment of provisional government in the country.

On 27 December 2019, former members of the Independent Police Union held a protest in Skopje, claiming their suspension of union membership by the finance ministry to be illegal and to affect their personal income.

On 3 March 2020, around 100 people, including politicians, trade unions and students' groups, rallied at Stirling University, Stirling to protest the suspension of 13 students who participated in a student-led occupation of university buildings last year.
On 15 March 2020, around 100 prisoners demonstrated in Metz against new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic.
On 16 March 2020, around 5000 workers of Mercedes Benz halt the production of the factory in Vitoria-Gasteiz and demanded the suspension of operations due to the coronavirus crisis outbreak.

On 17 March 2020, prisoners demonstrated in Paris against new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) as well as to show their fear of not being supplied in groceries due to the coronavirus epidemic. They refused to return to their cells on the way back from their walks and screamed for 30 minutes. They were heard by the whole neighborhood. It was settled peacefully.
On 17 March 2020, prisoners demonstrated in Maubeuge against new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic.

On 17 March 2020, around a 100 prisoners demonstrated and blocked their courtyard in Perpignan against new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic. After discussions with the management, they agreed to return to their cells half an hour later.

On 17 March 2020, 54 prisoners refused to return to their cells and demonstrated in Angers against new measures due to the coronavirus, including the suspension of social visits. The gendarmes and prison guards intervened. 2 prison guards were injured.

On 18 March 2020, about thirty prisoners demonstrated in Reau against new measures due to the coronavirus, including the suspension of social visits. Some equipment has been destroyed. Prison guards intervened. No one was injured.

On 19 March 2020, 14 prisoners demonstrated on the roof of their prison in Argentan to protest new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic. Police forces intervened to bring them down.
On 19 March 2020, prisoners demonstrated in Nantes against new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic.

On 19 March 2020, around 30 prisoners demonstrated and blocked their courtyard in Nanterre against new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic. Police intervened to stop the protest.
On 19 March 2020, prisoners demonstrated in Val-de-Reuil to protest new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic.
On 19 March 2020, prisoners demonstrated in Angers to protest new quarantine rules (suspension of visiting hours and external visits) due to the coronavirus epidemic.

As reported on 9 April 2020, about 40 bus drivers and conductors held a sponatenous protest in Tirana, after they found out they were dismissed, while waiting to receive their salaries amidst the coronavirus epidemic and suspension of public transport in the capital city.

On 20 April 2020, around 15 people staged a protest in Plebiscito square in Napoli (Napoli, Campania) while respecting social distancing policies to call on the Italian government to grant the citizenship income to a greater number of people. Protesters also asked for the suspension of rents and bills. The requests were made in view of the economic difficulties that emerged as a consequence of the spread of the coronavirus.

On 18 May 2020, restaurants and bar owners gathered outside the Town Hall in Cosenza (Cosenza, Calabria) to ask for liquidity to support their business and for a suspension in the payment of taxes amid the coronavirus emergency.

On 9 June 2020, teachers and unions gathered at the high school of Melle. They protested against the temporary suspension of three teachers that had protested against the education reform that was modifying the conditions of the baccalaureate.

On 16 June 2020, at the call of STC, CGT, and FO, between 1,300 and 2,500 health workers demonstrated from the train station to the prefecture in Ajaccio (Corse) as part of the national day of action for the defense of public hospitals. They also denounced coronavirus-related decrees and orders issued by the government as an attack on workers' rights (derogations to increase the working week to 60 hours, temporary reduction of rest periods, temporary suspension of paid vacations). They also asked for bonuses specific to Corsica, in particular the extension of the Corsican regional transport allowance (IRTC).

On 16 June 2020, around twenty people gathered outside the swimming pool of Busto Arsizio (Varese) to protest against the suspension of competitive swim classes due to the coronavirus crisis. The protesters claimed that coronavirus was an excuse because non-competitive classes started again.

On 16 June 2020, dozens of countryside festivals' workers blocked the traffic in Bari to protest against the festivals' suspension due to the coronavirus crisis. They currently don't have any income and they didn't receive the State's financial aid yet. The police intervened to ease the situation, but no clashes occurred.
On 21 June 2020, a group of people gathered with banners in Lecce for a musical flash mob in protest against the suspension of local festivals due to the coronavirus emergency.

On 24 June 2020, over 40 workers of the railway system gathered outside Mestre train station to protest against the suspension of their jobs due to the coronavirus crisis and to demand financial aids. The demonstration was organized by the Italian Labor unions CGIL, CISL and UIL.

On 26 June 2020, dozens of students staged a protest at the rectorship of the University of Pisa (Pisa, Toscana) and demanded clear answers about the reopening of the university after the coronavirus crisis. Protesters also asked a suspension of the payment of taxes since services were not provided during the coronavirus lockdown, and families were economically affected.

On 22 July 2020, workers of school transportation services protested in Malaga demanding that the regional government pays compensations for the suspension of contracts during the coronavirus crisis outbreak.

On 23 July 2020, in the evening, around 60 demonstrators climbed the Statue of justice in the Palud place of Lausanne. They protested at the call of several associations to demand the suspension of 6 police officers involved in the death of a man from Nigeria, who died during his arrest.

On 27 July 2020, workers of school transportation services protested in Sevilla demanding that the regional government pays compensations for the suspension of contracts during the coronavirus crisis outbreak. Protesters also demanded the prolongation of their contracts for the next school year (2020-2021).

On 27 July 2020, workers of school transportation services protested in Malaga demanding that the regional government pays compensations for the suspension of contracts during the coronavirus crisis outbreak. Protesters also demanded the prolongation of their contracts for the next school year (2020-2021).

On 17 August 2020, around 90 children, parents, grandparents, and one AfD politician protested in Rathenow outside a primary school against the suspension of the school director, who had criticised the obligatory wearing of masks.
On 21 August 2020, several hundred persons, including FO members, demonstrated in Paris. They protested against the suspension of a labor inspector.

On 27 August 2020, Extinction Rebellion activists blocked the Clifton Suspension Bridge in Bristol as part of a nationwide protest announcing the start of a rebellion and protesting government inaction on the climate and ecological emergency. Two female protesters were arrested.

On 1 September 2020, dozens of driving instructors staged a protest outside of a driving test centre in Craigavon, Co. Armagh over the suspension of driving test services, and the knock-on effects of job losses.
On 21 September 2020, workers of Xeal (former Ferroatlantica) gathered in Cee (A Coruna) to protest against the company's decision to implement a new temporary suspension of employment.

On 23 September 2020, workers of Xeal (former Ferroatlantica) gathered in front of the City Council of Cee (A Coruna) to protest against the company's decision to implement a new temporary suspension of employment.
On 24 September 2020, workers of Xeal (former Ferroatlantica) gathered in O Ezaro (A Coruna) to protest against the company's decision to implement a new temporary suspension of employment.
On 16 October 2020, around 150 students gathered un Granada to protest against the suspension of in-person classes at the university due to the growing number of coronavirus cases.

On 20 October 2020, Spreafico fruit-distributor workers gathered outside the company's warehouse in Dolzago (Lecco, Lombardia) and blocked the goods. Workers received letters of suspension for having taken part in a strike in September and that triggered the protest. Labor unions COBAS and S.I. COBAS were involved.

On 21 October 2020, Spreafico fruit-distributor workers gathered outside the company's warehouse in Dolzago (Lecco, Lombardia) and blocked the goods. Workers received letters of suspension for having taken part in a strike in September and that triggered the protest. Labor unions COBAS and S.I. COBAS were involved.

Around 25 October 2020 (week of), business owners staged a protest in Cava de' Tirreni (Salerno, Campania) to demand a suspension of taxes given that their businesses are affected by the coronavirus epidemic.
On 28 October 2020, hundreds of people, including a mayor from Our Party and at least one city councillor, protested in Vulcanesti against the suspension of the mayor's term.

On 28 October 2020, more than 100 people, including residents, shop owners, and restaurant owners, gathered in Roma Square in Ospitaletto (Lombardia) to protest against the government's latest special decree to curb the spread of coronavirus, which mandated early closure of restaurants and clubs at 6 PM, suspension of sports and cultural activities, and a curfew at 11 PM. Organized by Matteo Toto, a minority city councilor affiliated with Let's Take Ospitaletto Back (Riprendiamoci Ospitaletto), the protest was supported by local leaders from FdI, Lega, and FI. Protesters, under the slogan 'Let us work,' symbolically urged the government to allow safe business operations.

On 30 October 2020, demonstrators led by Boec blocked the access to the construction site for Turkish Stream the village of Rasovo (Medkovets), demanding the resignation of government and suspension of the project.

On 2 November 2020, around 50 women demonstrated in Geneva to call for the immediate suspension of a radio broadcaster's employee accused of harassment by a dozen employees. They also called for the launch of an internal investigation into the chain of responsibility. They protested also to empower women to break the silence and come forward to testify.

On 2 November 2020, women demonstrated in Fribourg to call for the immediate suspension of a radio broadcaster's employee accused of harassment by a dozen employees. They also called for the launch of an internal investigation into the chain of responsibility and denounced sexism. They protested also to empower women to break the silence and come forward to testify.

On 2 November 2020, around 35 women demonstrated in Lausanne to call for the immediate suspension of a radio broadcaster's employee accused of harassment by a dozen employees. They also called for the launch of an internal investigation into the chain of responsibility. They protested also to empower women to break the silence and come forward to testify.

On 2 November 2020, people and CGT union demonstrated in Narbonne against how much management is using subcontracting, which they feel is too prevalent. They denounced the suspension of employment contracts without pay and the mobilization of firefighters to replace them.
On 4 November 2020, lawyers protested in Burgas against the potential coronavirus measure that would result in the suspension of legal services.
On 4 November 2020, about twenty lawyers protested in Plovdiv against the potential coronavirus measure that would result in the suspension of legal services.
On 4 November 2020, lawyers protested in Sofia against the potential coronavirus measure that would result in the suspension of legal services
On 4 November 2020, lawyers protested in Varna against the potential coronavirus measure that would result in the suspension of legal services.

On 10 November 2020, students of the SzFE held a protest in Budapest VIII, announcing the suspension of the university's blockade, but reiterating the need for the reinstatement of the university's autonomy and public status. The students also voiced solidarity with the health workers. They also later protested outside the education office of the university demanding the continuation of the credit offering for courses taught during the university blockade.

On 12 November 2020, health workers gathered in Malaga to protest against the Regional Government's order of 9 November, regulating the suspension of holidays, licenses, and permits of the health care personnel during the coronavirus pandemic framework. The protest was supported by the General Union of Workers (UGT), Workers Commissions (CCOO), Public Official's Independent Trade Union (CSIF), and Nursing Union (SATSE).

On 12 November 2020, health workers gathered in Granada to protest against the Regional Government's order of 9 November, regulating the suspension of holidays, licenses, and permits of the health care personnel during the coronavirus pandemic framework. The protest was supported by the General Union of Workers (UGT) and Workers Commissions (CCOO).

On 19 November 2020, around 50 nursing students protested in Salon-de-Provence against a coronavirus-related measure concerning the suspension of their classes. They assumed it was to force them to help out in hospitals.

On 19 November 2020, health workers gathered in Malaga to protest against the Regional Government's order of 9 November, regulating the suspension of holidays, licenses, and permits of the health care personnel during the coronavirus pandemic framework. The protest was supported by the General Union of Workers (UGT), Nursing Union (SATSE), Malaga Medical Union (SMA) and Workers Commissions (CCOO).

On 19 November 2020, nurses in training, mainly women, demonstrated in front of the hospital in Martigues. They denounced the notification by email of the suspension of classes and internships, which was a decision made to make them available to help hospitals address the coronavirus outbreak.

On 19 November 2020, around 100 people and CGT union members demonstrated in Narbonne against how much management is using subcontracting, which they feel is too prevalent. They denounced the suspension of employment contracts without pay and the mobilization of firefighters to replace them which sabotages their strike. They also demonstrated against the conditions under which employees retire.

On 21 November 2020, a group of street and market vendors staged a sit-in in Bengasi square in Torino (Torino, Piemonte) to protest against the restrictions to their activity introduced by the government amidst a second wave of coronavirus infections and demand the suspension of taxes for their professional category.

On 24 November 2020, nearly 250 nurses in training demonstrated in front of the Regional Health Agency in Marseille at the call of the CGT union. They denounced the notification by email of the suspension of classes and internships, which was a decision made to make them available to help hospitals address the coronavirus outbreak.

On 26 November 2020, health workers gathered in the Valladolid University Clinical Hospital demanding the withdrawal of the Regional Government's decree about the 'reorganization' of health care personnel to cope with the coronavirus pandemic.They protested against staff mobility within the region and the suspension of holidays, licenses, and permits. The protest was supported by the General Union of Workers (UGT), Nursing Union (SATSE) and Workers Commissions (CCOO).

On 2 December 2020, around 80 employees of Orano and CGT union members demonstrated in Narbonne against how much management is using subcontracting, which they feel is too prevalent. They denounced the suspension of employment contracts without pay and the mobilization of firefighters to replace them which sabotages their strike. They also demonstrated against the conditions under which employees retire.

On 6 December 2020, around 80 parents accompanied by their children protested in Limoges against a bill that bans homeschooling. In addition to the suspension of the law, the demonstrators also called for fewer inspections if the first one goes well.

On 8 December 2020, around 80 employees of Orano and CGT union members demonstrated in Narbonne against how much management is using subcontracting, which they feel is too prevalent. They denounced the suspension of employment contracts without pay and the mobilization of firefighters to replace them which sabotages their strike. They also demonstrated against the conditions under which employees retire.

On 10 December 2020, health workers protested in Valladolid demanding the withdrawal of the Regional Government's decree about the 'reorganization' of health care personnel to cope with the coronavirus pandemic. They protested against staff mobility within the region and the suspension of holidays, licenses, and permits. The protest was supported by the State Confederation of Medical Unions (CESM), Nursing Union (SATSE), and Workers Commissions (CCOO).

On 17 December 2020, around 40 police officers and SGP Police-FO Unity union members protested in Tours to denounce an interview with the French president acknowledging the existence of police violence as well as racial profiling. They explained that it was false and unfair. They also protested against a police officer's six-month suspension and 18-month work ban for slapping a man in the face during an intervention.

On 18 December 2020, a group of transport ticket-selling Giroservice employees affiliated with USB Trasporti gathered in Napoli (Napoli, Campania) to protest against the suspension of services by their company amidst the coronavirus pandemic and denounce their resultant liquidation and lay-offs.

On 22 December 2020, around 50 health workers protested in Salamanca demanding the withdrawal of the Regional Government's decree about the 'reorganization' of health care personnel to cope with the coronavirus pandemic. They protested against staff mobility within the region and the suspension of holidays, licenses, and permits. The protest was organised by the General Union of Workers (UGT).

On 12 January 2021, the labor union COBAS organized a demonstration outside the Lecce Prefecture to protest against several things (not related to COVID-19): the suspension of the activities at the La Fontanella nursing home; the uncertainty of the jobs for educators of students with disabilities; the cut in working hours and personnel of Alba Service.

The issue was related to the controversial governmental reform of courts. The event was co-organized by the KOD movement.

On 18 January 2021, activists including judges gathered for a rally in Bialystok, demanding a resumption of duties of several other judges, who are currently under - allegedly wrongful - suspension. The issue was related to the controversial governmental reform of courts.

On 21 January 2021, a group of female legal gambling industry workers staged a flash mob in Montecitorio square in Rome - City Center (Roma, Lazio) to protest against the suspension of their activities decided by authorities amidst the second coronavirus wave. Protesters were dressed in black as a reported way to mourn their closed businesses.
On 26 January 2021, taxi drivers demonstrated in front of the city hall in Marseille. They demanded the suspension of the parking fee for 2020.

On 29 January 2021, nearly 300 persons, including professional artists, gathered at the Capitol square in Toulouse. They demonstrated to support the Mix'Art Myrys association, on the verge of closing following an administration closing and the suspension of its subventions.

On 3 February 2021, around 70 people gathered in Jennersdorf to protest against the suspension of a public health officer after she had participated and spoken at a protest against the coronavirus protection measures in Oberwart.

On 6 February 2021, around 1300 people (3000 according to the organizers) protested in Barcelona against the Constitutional Court's suspension of the Decree approved by the Parliament of Catalonia in 2019 to improve housing access. Protesters said that the decision leaves many families in a vulnerable position. The Platform for People Affected by Mortgages (PAH) called the protest.

On 19 February 2021, about 20 Cot Sicilian prison canteen service operators affiliated with CISL FISASCAT, CGIL FILMAN and UILTUCS took part in a sit-in in Cerulli square in Palermo (Palermo, Sicilia) to protest against the suspension of their activities following the replacement of their company as a canteen service provider and demand guarantees regarding their professional future.

On 19 February 2021, a group of Cot Sicilian prison canteen service operators affiliated with CISL FISASCAT took part in a sit-in outside the Cavadonna prison facility in Siracusa (Siracusa, Sicilia) to protest against the suspension of their activities following the replacement of their company as a canteen service provider and demand guarantees regarding their professional future.
On 26 February 2021, workers of the public postal service gathered in Leon to protest over work overload. They denounced the suspensions of staff recruitment.

On 2 March 2021, employees of the Aubert & Duval and Interforge demonstrated in front of the factories of both companies in Issoire. They denounced an announced job cut, a salary freeze and the suspension of any incentive compensation.

On 2 March 2021, employees of the Aubert & Duval and Interforge demonstrated in front of the factory of the company in Les Ancizes, at the call of the CGT union. They denounced an announced job cut, a salary freeze and the suspension of any incentive compensation.

On 3 March 2021, employees of the Aubert & Duval and Interforge demonstrated in front of the factory of the company in Les Ancizes, at the call of the CGT union. They denounced an announced job cut, a salary freeze and the suspension of any incentive compensation.

On 12 March 2021, workers of the public postal service gathered in Ferrol (A Coruna) to protest over work overload. They denounced the suspensions of staff recruitment. The Galician Inter-Union Confederation (CIG) called the protest.

On 11 April 2021, around 200 people waiting to get the COVID-19 vaccine outside San Camillo hospital in Manfredonia (Foggia, Puglia) started protesting against the suspension of the administration of vaccines due to the lack of doses.

On 20 April 2021, tour bus drivers at the wheel of dozens of vehicles rode and honked through the streets and later gathered outside the Campania Region headquarters in Napoli (Napoli, Campania) to lament the 'death' of their sector in the wake of the coronavirus emergency and ask for the suspension of the motor vehicle tax for their category as a relief measure. The protest, which was organized by the Campania branch of the AssoBus trade union association, created traffic disruptions.

On 29 April 2021, FILLEA CGIL, FILCA CISL and FENEAL UIL representatives staged a 'sit-in' protest at the Mortillaro channel construction site in Palermo (Palermo, Sicilia) to protest against the suspension of the work, denounce its impact on traffic and ask for the Municipality and the Sicilia Region to unfreeze it.

On 30 April 2021, between 300 and 500 people gathered, at the call of the health branch of CUB, in De Ferrari square in Genova (Genova, Liguria) to protest against the national government's decision to make vaccination against coronavirus compulsory for health workers. Protesters, some of them affiliated with the Democratic Medicine association, defended freedom of choice and the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines.
On 1 May 2021, around 130 people protested in Coburg on the occasion of May Day (Labor Day). The protesters demanded, amongst other, the suspension of patent rights for vaccines.

On 12 May 2021, FLAICA and CUB trade union affiliates gathered outside Gaetano Pini Hospital in Milano - Municipio 1 (Milano, Lombardia) to protest against the suspension and laying off of a fellow union delegate from the facility's cleaning and catering department in February 2021.

On 21 May 2021, health workers and other health facility employees gathered, at the call of the health branch of CUB, in Garibaldi square in Parma (Parma, Emilia-Romagna) to protest against the national government's decision to make vaccination against coronavirus compulsory for health workers. Protesters also criticized the privatization of healthcare and called for the recruitment of more health personnel and the suspension of 'numerus clausus' for nursing and medical faculties. The event was part of a national day of protest called by trade unions' health branches.

On 21 May 2021, workers affiliated with the health branch of the CUB trade union gathered in De Ferrari square in Genova (Genova, Liguria) to protest against the national government's decision to make vaccination against coronavirus compulsory for health workers. Protesters also criticized the privatization of healthcare and called for the recruitment of more health personnel and the suspension of 'numerus clausus' for nursing and medical faculties. The event was part of a national day of protest called by trade unions' health branches.

On 21 May 2021, health workers and USB health section trade unionists gathered in Cagliari (Cagliari, Sardegna) to demand universal and free public healthcare, a suspension of 'numerus clausus' for nursing and medicine faculties, the recruitment of additional health personnel and call for a waiver on coronavirus vaccines' patents. The event was part of a national movement of strike and protest called by USB on the day of the G20 Global Health Summit in Rome.

On 21 May 2021, health workers and USB health section trade unionists gathered in Indipendenza square in Palermo (Palermo, Sicilia) to demand universal and free public healthcare, a suspension of 'numerus clausus' for nursing and medicine faculties, the recruitment of additional health personnel and call for a waiver on coronavirus vaccines' patents. The event, to which a No G20 delegation from Catania also took part, was part of a national movement of strike and protest called by USB on the day of the G20 Global Health Summit in Rome.

Around 25 May 2021 (as reported), workers of various cultural Apulian landmarks employed by the Ministry of Culture gathered outside Castel del Monte in Andria (Puglia) to protest against the suspension of some of their contracts, stressing their many years of service.

On 29 May 2021, CGIL, CISL, and UIL members gathered in Rossi square in Catanzaro under the banner 'No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the temporary suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines and drugs. They also collected signatures.

On 29 May 2021, CGIL, CISL, and UIL members gathered in Crotone under the banner 'No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the temporary suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines and drugs. They also collected signatures.

On 29 May 2021, CGIL, CISL, and UIL members gathered in Municipio square in Vibo Valentia under the banner 'No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the temporary suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines and drugs. They also collected signatures.

On 29 May 2021, as part of the 'Right2Cure - No Profit On Pandemic' European campaign and at the call of dozens of unspecified Italian entities, including trade unions, people gathered in Torino (Piemonte) to call for the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines in order to produce vaccines for economically backward countries.

On 29 May 2021, people gathered in Florence (Firenze, Toscana) under the banner 'Right2Cure - No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines in order to produce vaccines for economically backward countries.

On 29 May 2021, people gathered in Rome (Roma, Lazio) under the banner 'Right2Cure - No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines in order to produce vaccines for economically backward countries.

On 29 May 2021, people gathered in Palermo under the banner 'Right2Cure - No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines in order to produce vaccines for economically backward countries.

On 29 May 2021, people gathered in Argentina square in Milano - Municipio 3 under the banner 'Right2Cure - No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines in order to produce vaccines for economically backward countries.

On 29 May 2021, people gathered in Duca d'Aosta square in Milano - Municipio 2 under the banner 'Right2Cure - No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines in order to produce vaccines for economically backward countries.

On 29 May 2021, people gathered in Napoli under the banner 'Right2Cure - No Profit On Pandemic' to demand the suspension of patents on coronavirus vaccines in order to produce vaccines for economically backward countries.

On 22 June 2021, around 40 activists of No Profit On Pandemic staged a protest in front of the Belgian Federal Parliament in Brussels - Ville de Bruxelles to demand a suspension of the patents on the coronavirus vaccine. Among the protesters, members of Ecolo, PS and PVDA-PTB were present.
On 1 August 2021, Legambiente activists gathered in Punta Penna shore in Vasto (Chieti, Abruzzo) to protest against the suspension of the construction of a wind farm and solar power system.
On 2 August 2021, residents of Lumas protested in Lumas, demanding the suspension of the water supply system from the Sinec spring. The protesters were dispersed with tear gas by the police.

On 4 August 2021, residents of Lumas protested in Lumas, demanding the suspension of the water supply system from the Sinec spring, the dismissal of two police directors, and a meeting with the Albanian prime-minister.

On 9 August 2021, No Vax activists gathered outside the Bolzano courthouse to protest against the suspension of two nurses who didn't want to get the coronavirus vaccines. The demonstration took place while the nurses were having the judicial hearing.

On 23 August 2021, paramedics and employees of the EKAV ambulance service rallied in Thessaloniki against the suspension of unvaccinated colleagues, which would come into force as of 1 September, as the government would tighten measures in healthcare to prevent the spread of the coronavirus. The protesters called the government to withdraw the bill or freeze it to give time for more dialogue.

On 1 September 2021, health care workers rallied outside a major Thessaloniki hospital against mandatory coronavirus vaccination in their sector following a third surge of coronavirus infections in the country. Unvaccinated health workers facing suspension confronted verbally TV crews reporting on the protest.

On 2 September 2021, health care workers rallied outside the Ministry of Macedonia and Thrace in Thessaloniki to protest against mandatory coronavirus vaccination in their sector, following a third surge of coronavirus infections in the country and the suspension of about 6,000 unvaccinated frontline health workers as of 1 September. The demonstrators were organized by POEDIN, which called a Panhellenic strike-protest.

On 2 September 2021, about 400 hundred health care workers, accompanied by ambulances with sirens blaring, rallied outside the health ministry in Athens - Central Athens before marching to parliament to protest against mandatory coronavirus vaccination in their sector, following a third surge of coronavirus infections in the country and the suspension of about 6,000 unvaccinated frontline health workers as of 1 September. On 28 December 2022, around 100 volunteers, health workers and 15 ambulances staged a demonstration in Campobasso (Campobasso, Molise) to protest against the possible suspension of the emergency transport service.

On 22 January 2023, over 7,000 citizens and shopkeepers held a protest in Murcia (Murcia) against the mobility plans of the Murcia City Council and demand their suspension. Representatives of PP and Vox attended the protest.

On 25 March 2023, people, called by the United Neighborhoods for Sustainable Mobility, held a protest march from Alameda de Colon to Avenida de la Libertad in Murcia (Murcia) against the mobility plans of the Murcia City Council and demand their suspension.

On 27 April 2023, over 200 firefighters held a protest in Aranda del Duero (Burgos, Castilla y Leon) to support a colleague whom the City Council intends to sanction with seven months suspension of employment and salary.

On 3 May 2023, between 200 and 300 people gathered in front of the PSG headquarters in Boulogne-Billancourt to denounce the football team's management following their recent loss of a game, and the suspension of Lionel Messi following his unauthorized trip to Saudi Arabia.

On 13 November 2023, just over 10 KOZP and XR activists occupied the municipal council chamber of Elburg (Gelderland) for two hours in a sit-in against Black Pete (Zwarte Piet) in Sinterklaas celebrations, a tradition that included painting faces black during St. Nicholas' Eve, calling it racist and demanding an end to it. They held a banner reading 'Memorial year of slavery past: no Black Pete in Elburg.' The mayor repeatedly asked them to move to the public gallery, but they refused, leading to a suspension of the meeting.

On 14 December 2023, about 100 medical students, and health workers, staged a picket outside the Polyclinic in Bari (Puglia) to protest against the competent authorities' decision to apply (on 11 December, without previous communications and after five years of suspension) a Memorandum of Understanding that limits internships to students with specific qualifications. On 11 December, many trainees previously working at the Polyclinic were dismissed and lost their internships. Representatives of Link, members of the student association 'Studenti Per', and city councilor Massimiliano Carbonara (PD) were present.
On 30 December 2023, several people, including teachers, protested outside the Fine Arts Museum in Budapest - XIV, condemning the suspension of teaching of history of arts in schools.

On 31 December 2023, an unknown number of Villaservice's workers demonstrated in Villacidro (Sardegna) by climbing on the structures of the company's plant, which manages waste collection in the area, to demand certainties about their jobs and protest against the planned suspension of 40 workers at the beginning of the new year.

On 27 January 2024, at the call of Dignified Public Transport Platform (Plataforma Transporte Publico Digno), nearly 100 people protested in Ribeira (A Coruna, Galicia) to demand an end to public transport problems such as delays, suspension of lines, and non-compliance with schedules.

On 14 February 2024, students rallied in the rectorate of the National and Kapodistrian University of Athens in Athens - Central Athens, to protest against the suspension of a member of the university's admin staff, who shut down a computer server, preventing students from completing their exams online the previous day, in solidarity with protesting students over the establishment of private universities in Greece.
On 14 February 2024, MSZP youth members held a similar protest outside the Diakhitel loan company in Budapest - II, condemning the suspension of certain loans for students.

On 19 February 2024, at the call of 'Initiative Star Bridge' (Initiative Sternbrucke), between 180 (according to the police) and 200 people (according to a reporter) participated in a demonstration in Hamburg - Altona (Hamburg) against the demolition of the historic Sternbrucke. They called for suspension of construction work for four weeks. At the end of the demonstration, some participants refused to leave the road. Police forces intervened to clear the road and scuffles broke out, leading to one police officer being assaulted. One demonstrator was temporarly arrested. Two others started a sit-in and were forcefully carried away.

On 24 February 2024, at the call of RSU and High School Students' Network, more than 500 people, including students, staged a picket outside the Secondary School 'Barozzi' in Modena (Emilia-Romagna) to ask for the revocation of the suspension of a student representative who had granted an interview considered defamatory by the school's Council. Representatives of the 'Press Association Modena' (Associazione Stampa Modena) made of journalists, teachers, and exponents of the administration were present.

On 24 February 2024, at the call of Europe for peace, people staged a picket in Margherita Gardens in Piacenza (Emilia-Romagna) to demand the end of the Russian-Ukraine war, the suspension of sending weapons to Ukraine, and an immediate ceasefire.

On 8 March 2024, people staged a protest in Caltanissetta (Sicilia) against the isolation created by the suspension of the road works implemented by the CMC company. The mayor of Aragona Giuseppe Pendolino (civic list), of Caltanissetta Giovanni Ruvolo (civic list), of Agrigento Lillo Firetto (civic list), the President of the Municipal Council Gioacchino Volpe, the bishop of Caltanissetta Mario Russotto and trade unions were present.

On 25 March 2024, more than 100 journalists and employees, including members of SNG-CSE, gathered outside the La Provence headquarters in Marseille (Provence-Alpes-Cote d'Azur) to protest against the suspension of the editorial director of the La Provence group, who was accused of being complacent with drug dealers. Demonstrators called for greater independence in their work, accusing local and national authorities of interfering in newspaper content.

On 3 April 2024, at the call of FIALS, health workers staged a protest in Montescaglioso Street in Matera (Basilicata) against the suspension of the activities of a local geriatric center and the collective dismissal of workers.

On 23 April 2024, several hundred people held a protest in Leon (Castilla y Leon) against the celebration in Leon of the events of the Day of the Community of Castilla y Leon, and to call for self-determination and more self-governance. Protesters threw crackers leading to the suspension of the official events. Members of UPL and the mayor of Leon (PSOE) took part in the protest.

On 13 June 2024, at the call of the 'Coordination of Tenants Committees of Pio Albergo Trivulzio' (Coordinamento dei Comitati degli Inquilini), SUNIA, 'Home and territory tenants union' (SICET), 'National Union of Tenants, Environment and Territory National APS' (UNIAT), and 'Tenants Union' (Unione Inquilini), people demonstrated in Scala Square in Milano - Municipio 1 (Lombardia) against the sale of properties owned by the retirement home and hospital Pio Albergo Trivulzio to a private fund. Protesters demanded the suspension of all eviction processes and the halting of termination notices sent to tenants.

On 22 June 2024, thousands of people gathered in Cork (Cork) to show their solidarity with Natasha O'Brien and call for an end to gender-based violence. She was assaulted by a serving member of the Defense Forces, who was consequently given a three-year suspension. Sinn Fein and Labour Party councilors also attended the protest.

On 22 June 2024, about 500 people gathered in Galway (Galway) to show their solidarity with Natasha O'Brien and call for an end to gender-based violence. She was assaulted by a serving member of the Defense Forces, who was consequently given a three-year suspension.

On 22 June 2024, thousands of people gathered in Limerick (Limerick City and County) to show their solidarity with Natasha O'Brien and call for an end to gender-based violence. She was assaulted by a serving member of the Defense Forces, who was consequently given a three-year suspension. Ms. O'Brien also joined the demonstration.

On 25 June 2024, hundreds of people gathered outside the Parliament in Dublin (Dublin) to show their solidarity with Natasha O'Brien and call for an end to gender-based violence. She was assaulted by a serving member of the Defense Forces, who was consequently given a three-year suspension. Natasha O'Brien also attended the protest and addressed the crowd.

On 25 June 2024, more than 200 people gathered in Manorhamilton (Leitrim) to show their solidarity with Natasha O'Brien and call for an end to gender-based violence. She was assaulted by a serving member of the Defense Forces, who was consequently given a three-year suspension.

On 27 June 2024, upward of 60 people gathered outside Washington Street Courthouse in Cork (Cork) to show their solidarity with Natasha O'Brien and call for an end to gender-based violence. She was assaulted by a serving member of the Defense Forces, who was consequently given a three-year suspension. Natasha O'Brien also attended the protest, which was organized by ROSA, and addressed the crowd.

On 23 July 2024, around 150 people, including street artists, protested in Genova (Liguria) against a new regulation for street performances introduced by the City Council. The demonstration caused significant disruption, leading to the suspension of the council session.

On 5 August 2024, about twenty Platone Institute's students and parents staged a protest in front of the Regional School Office in Giovanni Fattori Street in Palermo (Sicilia) against the suspension of the tests, following inspections, for 82 students who attended lessons online.

On 19 September 2024, sailors and dockers from La Meridionale gathered in Ajaccio (Corse) to protest against the imminent suspension of the L'Ile-Rousse-Toulon shipping service. They called for an open strike from 1 October 2024, denouncing a blow to 'territorial continuity'.

On 20 September 2024, at the call of Fim CISL, Rmp Savoini workers staged a picket outside the factory gates in Galliate (Piemonte) to protest against the suspension of production pending a proceeding for bankruptcy and demand certainties about the workers' future.

On 22 September 2024, people gathered in Den Haag (Zuid-Holland) to protest against a new law banning face-covering during protests in the Netherlands. They argued that face coverings were necessary to protect their identities due to potential repercussions, such as suspension from school or sanctions from employers, faced by recognizable protesters.

On 15 October 2024, activists brought posters, pieces of wired fence and candles during the night in front of offices of local deputies to the Polish senate in Gdansk, protesting against the suspension of asylum rights in the country.

On 22 October 2024, activists, including ORP members, gathered for a rally in front of the Prime Minister's office in Warsaw, protesting against the suspension of the asylum right in the country. Among the protesters, there was also Agnieszka Holland, an awarded director of documents about human rights violations and violence against refugees at the Polish-Belarusian border.
On 22 October 2024, ORP activists gathered for a rally in Wroclaw, protesting against the suspension of the asylum right in the country.
On 24 October 2024, students of the Valle del Cidacos school held a protest in Calahorra (La Rioja) to denounce the lack of teaching staff leading to the suspension of courses.

On 2 December 2024, members of the Georgian community in Estonia demonstrated outside the Georgian Embassy in Kesklinn, Tallinn, to protest the Georgian government's suspension of the EU accession process and to denounce what they viewed as the government's anti-Western policies.

On 7 December 2024, hundreds of residents, along with local environmental activists, protested near Kralupi demanding the urgent suspension of renovation work on small hydropower plants along the Kraljuscica River due to concerns that the work might trigger landslides.

On 10 December 2024, people, including the mayor of Capri (civic list) and members of the municipal administration, staged a protest in Capri (Campania) to oppose the suspension of the local funicular service. The protesters demanded to be involved in decisions regarding the suspension, the planned works, and the timetable of the interventions.

On 18 December 2024, dozens of citizens organized a protest against the reduction of the territory of the 'Blue Stones' Nature Park in front of the regional administration building in Sliven. The protesters demand the preservation of the integrity of the Nature Park, the suspension of the procedure for removing 12 acres from its borders, where the building of the Alpine House is located.

On 18 December 2024, students gathered in front of the Faculty of Pedagogy in Jagodina as part of the 'Serbia, Stop' campaign, encouraging participants to pause their activities and block roads for 15 minutes to honor the 15 victims of the Novi Sad tragedy and demand accountability for their loss of life. That same day, the Faculty issued a statement announcing the suspension of classes to support the students' demands. These demands included the publication of documents related to the reconstruction of the Railway Station in Novi Sad, the release and dismissal of charges against those arrested during the protests, the filing of criminal charges against individuals who attacked students during the protests, and a 20% increase in funding for state universities.

On 19 December 2024, 35 teachers, including members of the union Action & Democracy (Action & Democratie) (affiliated with CFE-CGC), staged a picket line outside the Ettore-Bugatti vocational high school in Illzach (Grand Est) to protest the lenient disciplinary response to a student who threw a chair at a teacher, resulting in the student's suspension with a probation period. The teachers demanded stricter disciplinary measures to ensure staff safety and highlighted deteriorating school conditions, including heating failures, and overdue infrastructure improvements.

On 20 December 2024, citizens and activists organized by the Center for the Protection and Study of Birds (CZIP) held a protest in front of the Ministry of Spatial Planning, Urbanism, and State Property in Podgorica. They demanded the suspension of the adoption of the Spatial-Urban Plan (PUP) for Podgorica and called for a new process that would allow for democratic debate and the protection of citizens' rights and natural resources. The protesters expressed concern that the current procedure poses serious risks of corruption and violates the rule of law.

On 20 December 2024, education workers and students protested in Nis, including union representatives, demanding the suspension of the government's decision to end school semesters earlier due to ongoing occupations of schools and faculties.

On 21 December 2024, at the call of the association Audacity (Audace), around 700 people gathered in Mayenne (Pays de la Loire) to protest the temporary suspension of deliveries at the city's maternity hospital due to a shortage of anesthesiologists. Protesters expressed frustration over the strain on public healthcare services and the impact on expectant mothers, as approximately 40 women had to travel to other cities to give birth.

On 9 January 2025, around 30 protesters, organized by the Lewes Green Party, gathered outside East Sussex County Council to oppose the potential suspension of the county elections in May. The protesters held signs like 'RIP democracy' and brought a mock coffin to symbolize their concerns.

On 20 January 2025, a group of several tens of citizens gathered in front of the 'Vuk Karadzic' school in Surcin to protest the strike and work suspension announced by the teachers and education workers at the school. They directed insults at the school's principal and demanded to be allowed entry to the school's premises.

On 20 January 2025, teachers, researchers, and other staff members of the University of Nis marched in protest and gathered in front of the Faculty of Medicine in Nis. They were protesting a deal that some unions had made with the government, which included a salary raise of education workers, and instead expressed their support for an ongoing nationwide strike and suspension of schools. The strike itself aims to show solidarity with the students who are occupying faculties across the country demanding accountability for the loss of 15 lives resulting from the collapse of a concrete canopy at the train station in Novi Sad, attributing the tragedy to corruption. The students who are occupying the building in Nis had locked the entrance to prevent the January exam period from taking place.

On 21 January 2025, parents gathered in front of the 'Vuk Karadzic' school in Surcin to protest an incident that had occurred the day before when a group of citizens had insulted the school's principal and demanded access to the school's premises, opposing the strike and work suspension announced by teachers and education workers.

On 21 January 2025, high school students, along with their parents, teachers and citizens gathered in front of several gymnasiums in Belgrade to protest the education inspection announced in response to the ongoing strike by teachers and education workers, which had led to the suspension of classes. The strike was prompted by education workers opposing a deal that certain unions had negotiated with the government, which included salary increases. They believe it is inappropriate to benefit financially from the tragedy that occurred in Novi Sad. Instead, the workers chose to strike to express solidarity with the students occupying faculties across the country and to advocate for their demands to be addressed.

On 23 January 2025, striking Pam Panorama workers staged a picket outside the 'I Gigli' mall in Campi Bisenzio (Toscana) to protest against the company's decision to place several workers into unpaid suspension. All suspended workers are over 50 years old or part of protected categories. Representatives of FILCAMS CGIL, USB, and municipal labor councilor (Civic List) participated in the protest.

On 23 January 2025, around 400 citizens, teachers, and high school students gathered in front of the Gymnasium and Economic School in Gornji Milanovac to protest and prevent the education inspection to enter the school in response to the ongoing strike by teachers and education workers, which had led to the suspension of classes. The strike was prompted by education workers opposing a deal that certain unions had negotiated with the government, which included salary increases. They believe it is inappropriate to benefit financially from the tragedy that occurred in Novi Sad. Instead, the workers chose to strike to express solidarity with the students occupying faculties across the country and to advocate for their demands to be addressed.

On 23 January 2025, several hundred citizens, teachers, high school students, and labour union representatives gathered in front of the Gymnasiums in Cacak to protest and prevent the education inspection to enter the school in response to the ongoing strike by teachers and education workers, which had led to the suspension of classes. The strike was prompted by education workers opposing a deal that certain unions had negotiated with the government, which included salary increases. They believe it is inappropriate to benefit financially from the tragedy that occurred in Novi Sad. Instead, the workers chose to strike to express solidarity with the students occupying faculties across the country and to advocate for their demands to be addressed.

On 4 February 2025, in the afternoon, around 100 protesters, including members of the Congolese community, gathered at Jean Reyplein in the European district of Brussels - Ville de Bruxelles (Brussels). The demonstration, organized by Free Congo Belgique, called for immediate EU sanctions against Rwanda and an end to its support for the rebel group M23 in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Protesters denounced the European Union's 'complicity' in the violence and exploitation of Congo's resources, urging the suspension of all economic agreements with Rwanda.

On 7 February 2025, citizens gathered at various locations in Novi Sad to participate in the 'Serbia, Stop' campaign and pay tribute to 15 people who lost their lives after the canopy collapsed at the railway station in Novi Sad. Protesters also aimed to show support for students who are occupying their faculties, demanding accountability for this tragic event, attributing it to corruption. Employees of the Railway Workers' Health Protection Institute (ZZZZR 'Zeleznice Srbije') gathered in front of their workplace in Novi Sad, alongside bikers, to observe 15 minutes of silence. They additionally protested against the pressures they have been facing from their director for holding these memorial gatherings, with some even facing suspensions for their participation. Health workers and students gathered in front of the Clinical Center of Vojvodina to honor the victims.

On 7 February 2025, parents gathered outside the 'Jovan Popovic' Elementary School in Novi Sad to demand greater safety for their children. Their concerns were heightened by a recent incident involving a dead rabbit which somebody had left on the school's premises. This was perceived as a threat and an attempt at intimidation, especially since the rabbit symbol has been associated with previous protests in Novi Sad. Additionally, the parents expressed their opposition to a newly adopted teaching model that calls for a partial suspension of classes, as they believe it discriminates and divides students and staff, as some advocate for a complete suspension of classes while others continue to attend.

On 13 February 2025, citizens gathered in front of the Sofia Municipality to protest the suspension of two nighttime public transport lines. Protesters held posters with inscriptions such as 'Sofia needs night public transport,' 'Sofia does not go to bed at 12 o'clock,' and 'I need safe transport.'
On 13 February 2025, students blocked access to the Racine school in Paris (Ile-de-France) to demand the suspension of a teacher accused of hitting and insulting students.

On 15 February 2025, around 400 people, including PP and Vox elected officials, residents and environmentalists, protested in front of the Ministry of Industry in Oviedo (Asturias) to demand the suspension of battery park projects in Asturias. They argued that the projects posed risks such as excessive noise, potential fires, and negative impacts on children in nearby schools and on livestock.

On 17 February 2025, students of the Teatro Biondo school gathered in front of the theater's main entrance in Palermo (Sicilia) to demonstrate against the indefinite suspension of their training due to a leadership transition at the institution, organizing self-managed lessons in the street.

On 24 February 2025, about 100 people, including students of the Serpieri High School, teachers, and parents, demonstrated in Rimini (Emilia-Romagna) to protest a teacher's suspension and demand a change in the administration's management style.

On 26 February 2025, parents of school children, along with teachers, gathered in front of the 'United Nations' Elementary School in Cerak neighborhood of Belgrade - Cukarica to support teachers who decided to participate in class suspensions to support student-led protests. These protests demand accountability for the loss of 15 lives following the collapse of a concrete canopy at the Novi Sad railway station.

On 26 February 2025, education workers gathered in Kikinda to protest against announced salary cuts and other forms of pressure on striking teachers who are supporting student-led demonstrations and participate in ongoing class suspensions. These protests demand accountability for the loss of 15 lives following the collapse of a concrete canopy at the Novi Sad railway station.

On 26 February 2025, parents of school children, along with citizens, gathered in front of the 'Jovan Popovic' Elementary School in Novi Sad to support striking teachers and protest against announced salary cuts and other forms of pressure on teachers who participate in ongoing class suspensions to support student-led protests. These protests demand accountability for the loss of 15 lives following the collapse of a concrete canopy at the Novi Sad railway station. Following the school protest, participants joined a demonstration of lawyers and education workers in front of the Basic Court in Novi Sad (coded separately).

On 3 March 2025, teachers and citizens gathered in front of the 'Branko Radicevic' elementary school in Novi Sad to protest against pressures on striking teachers who are supporting student-led demonstrations and participate in ongoing class suspensions. These protests demand accountability for the loss of 15 lives following the collapse of a concrete canopy at the Novi Sad railway station.

On 4 March 2025, several dozen journalism students and members of NT blocked access to the ESJ journalism school in Lille (Hauts-de-France) to protest the institution's decision to suspend its agreement with NT following a controversy. The dispute arose after the group accused the school of tolerating 'rape culture' due to the participation of controversial lawyer Guillaume de Palma in a conference on a high-profile rape trial. The ESJ justified its decision by citing defamatory and insulting statements made by the group. Protesters argued that NT played a crucial role in addressing gender-based violence within the school and expressed concerns that the suspension could lead to its dissolution.

On 4 March 2025, citizens gathered in Sombor to protest against salary reductions and other forms of pressure on striking teachers who are supporting student-led demonstrations and participate in ongoing class suspensions.

On 5 March 2025, around 100 parents of school children, along with teachers, gathered in front of the 'Gornja varos' elementary school in Belgrade - Zemun to protest against announced salary cuts and other forms of pressure on striking teachers who are supporting student-led demonstrations and participating in ongoing class suspensions. These protests demand accountability for the loss of 15 lives following the collapse of a concrete canopy at the Novi Sad railway station.

On 13 March 2025, student associations organized education sector rallies with the participation of teachers. At noon, students rallied and then marched to Syntagma square demanding political accountability and justice for the victims of the 2023 Tempe rail disaster. Police detained 6 people during security checks before the march. The protest continued in the evening, when a large crowd rallied at Syntagma square, Athens - Central Athens, for a concert. Security measures, such as street closures and the temporary suspension of service at key stations on the metro, were put in place.

On 20 March 2025, people, including family members of people affected by severe disabilities and members of the local committees 'Disabled People of Torre Committee' (Comitato Disabili Torre) and 'The Dragonfly' (La Libellula), demonstrated in Torre del Greco (Campania) to protest the suspension of the allowances intended to support families and caregivers of people affected by severe disabilities take care of their loved ones. Local and national representatives of FdI, including national FdI spokesperson Raffaele Barbato and MEP Chiara Gemma, participated in the demonstration.

On 26 March 2025, a large group of people, including parents, gathered outside a work site for the construction of a nursery school in Palermo (Sicilia) to protest the suspension of the construction and demand answers concerning the project.

On 26 March 2025, in the morning, five activists from the initiative St. Marx for Everyone (St. Marx fur Alle) disrupted a session of the Vienna City Council in Wien - Landstrasse (Wien) to protest the planned construction of the Wien Holding Arena in St. Marx. The activists threw flyers and held up protest banners from the public gallery, forcing a temporary suspension of the meeting. They denounced the project's use of public funds, the loss of public space, and the lack of genuine public participation in the planning process.

On 29 March 2025, around 5,000 people, including health workers, elected officials from PSOE and IU, and opposition councilors in the City Council, held a protest outside the hospital in Calahorra (La Rioja) to denounce the regional government's layoff plan, arguing it would reduce the number of specialized medical staff. Demonstrators called for the reversal of the planned staffing cuts, the suspension of the new job restructuring scheme (RPT), and the full restoration of services and medical personnel at the hospital.

On 1 April 2025, in the morning, at the call of PAH, residents and activists held a protest in Sancibrian (Cantabria) to oppose the eviction of a 77-year-old woman and her son from their home. The protest took place as a large police operation carried out the eviction despite interim measures from the UN Committee on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights requesting its suspension. The demonstration proceeded without police intervention.

On 2 April 2025, around 100 people gathered outside the Regional Health Agency in Pau (Nouvelle-Aquitaine) to express support for a midwife facing a five-month suspension after a report by the Bayonne hospital following complications during a home birth. Protesters denounced what they considered an attack on home births and criticized the suspension as unfair, arguing it left the midwife without income and the ability to defend herself.

On 4 April 2025, in the afternoon, around 15 parents protested loudly in front of the Egied van Broeckhoven school in Brussels - Molenbeek-Saint-Jean (Brussels) against what they denounce as the school's excessive use of student suspensions. They accused the director of ignoring parental concerns and acting arbitrarily, citing several recent expulsions.

On 12 April 2025, in the afternoon, between 800 and 1,000 people, including members of the Catholic community, marched from Klostergarten to Domplatz in Passau (Bayern) to protest the suspension of a priest. They demanded his reinstatement, called for a more compassionate and relatable clergy, and criticized the bishop of Passau's handling of the case. The protest followed the priest's removal over allegations related to his youth work, including alcohol use, which did not lead to criminal charges.

On 23 April 2025, in the afternoon, at the call of the SOS Calahorra Hospital Platform (plataforma SOS hospital de Calahorra), people held a protest at the health center in Alfaro (La Rioja) to denounce the regional government's layoff plan. Protesters argued that the restructuring would reduce the number of specialized medical staff and demanded the reversal of staffing cuts, the suspension of the new job restructuring plan (RPT), and the full restoration of services and medical personnel at the hospital.

On 23 April 2025, in the afternoon, at the call of the SOS Calahorra Hospital Platform (plataforma SOS hospital de Calahorra), people held a protest at the health center in Cervera del Rio Alhama (La Rioja) to denounce the regional government's layoff plan. Protesters argued that the restructuring would reduce the number of specialized medical staff and demanded the reversal of staffing cuts, the suspension of the new job restructuring plan (RPT), and the full restoration of services and medical personnel at the hospital.

On 28 April 2025, in the morning, at the call of hospital staff, around 120 health workers gathered outside Emile Roux Hospital in Le Puy-en-Velay (Auvergne-Rhone-Alpes) to protest the treatment of seven nurses recently transferred from the endoscopy unit. Demonstrators demanded the suspension of a doctor accused of moral harassment by the nurses, who remains in post pending investigations by the Medical Council and the public prosecutor. Protesters denounced the hospital's decision to relocate the nurses rather than address the alleged misconduct.

On 30 April 2025, at the call of Ecologists in Action and the Conxo Aberto community association (Asociacion Vecinal Conxo Aberto), people held a protest in the Banquete de Conxo forest in Santiago de Compostela (Galicia) to demand a halt to nearby construction works linked to the Eixo de Mobilidade Sostible project. Protesters denounced the ongoing damage to the riverside forest, a UNESCO World Heritage site, despite an ongoing European anti-corruption investigation and judicial proceedings. They called for the immediate suspension of the works to prevent further irreversible harm.

On 7 May 2025, at the call of OGBL, protesters gathered outside the Chamber of Deputies in Luxembourg (Luxembourg) to protest about the suspension of four Caritas Jeunes & Familles staff delegates by management. The union denounced the decision, pointing to failures in the regulatory framework.

On 9 May 2025, there was a mass mobilization of solidarity protesters, teachers from Piraeus and Attica, students and workers, organized by their associations, who protested outside the Piraeus Court of Appeal against the suspension of a teacher refusing teachers' evaluations. The protest was successful and the teacher would return to school.

On 12 May 2025, in the morning, around 150 residents of Lomo Negro protested outside the town hall in Arona (Canarias) to oppose the planned demolitions of their homes, built on non-urbanizable land. The demonstrators demanded a definitive political solution to secure their housing. The demonstration followed the temporary suspension of two demolition orders by the Canary Islands Environmental Protection Agency due to safety concerns linked to expected protests.

On 15 May 2025, at the provincial call of the teaching community, parents' associations, and unions, people protested outside the Pare Victoria school in Alcoy (Valencia) to demand the reactivation of the Edificant plan, aimed at funding the reparation and adaptation of education centers lacking adequate infrastructure. The demonstration denounced the suspension of urgently needed repair and construction works in public schools across the region, criticizing the current regional government's inaction.

On 15 May 2025, at the provincial call of the teaching community, parents' associations, and unions, people gathered outside the Enric Valor school in El Campello (Valencia) to demand the reactivation of the Edificant plan, aimed at funding the reparation and adaptation of education centers lacking adequate infrastructure. The demonstration denounced the suspension of urgently needed repair and construction works in public schools across the region, criticizing the current regional government's inaction.

On 15 May 2025, at the provincial call of the teaching community, parents' associations, and unions, people protested outside the Blasco Ibanez school in Elche (Valencia) to demand the reactivation of the Edificant plan, aimed at funding the reparation and adaptation of education centers lacking adequate infrastructure. The demonstration denounced the suspension of urgently needed repair and construction works in public schools across the region, criticizing the current regional government's inaction.

On 15 May 2025, at the provincial call of the teaching community, parents' associations, and unions, people gathered outside the IES La Nucia in La Nucia (Valencia) to demand the reactivation of the Edificant plan, aimed at funding the reparation and adaptation of education centers lacking adequate infrastructure. The demonstration denounced the suspension of urgently needed repair and construction works in public schools across the region, criticizing the current regional government's inaction.

On 15 May 2025, at the provincial call of the teaching community, parents' associations, and unions, people protested outside the Los Espenetes school in Orihuela (Valencia) to demand the reactivation of the Edificant plan, aimed at funding the reparation and adaptation of education centers lacking adequate infrastructure. The demonstration denounced the suspension of urgently needed repair and construction works in public schools across the region, criticizing the current regional government's inaction.

On 15 May 2025, at the provincial call of the teaching community, parents' associations, and unions, people protested outside the San Vicente Ferrer school in Teulada (Valencia) to demand the reactivation of the Edificant plan, aimed at funding the reparation and adaptation of education centers lacking adequate infrastructure. The demonstration denounced the suspension of urgently needed repair and construction works in public schools across the region, criticizing the current regional government's inaction.

On 21 May 2025, in the morning, at the call of the European Works Council of ArcelorMittal, around 100 delegates from Luxembourg, Belgium, Germany, and France, including from OGBL, gathered in front of ArcelorMittal's headquarters in Luxembourg (Luxembourg), staging a protest picket to oppose the planned relocation of support functions to India and Poland. Protesters denounced the strategy threatening between 1,450 and 1,825 jobs across Europe amid the steel crisis, and demanded the immediate suspension of the process pending transparent consultation.

Around 22 May 2025 (week of), nurses protested outside Txagorritxu Hospital in Vitoria-Gasteiz (Pais Vasco) to oppose the digital hiring system of the Basque health system, citing errors in the staffing lists, lack of interim contract calls, and the suspension of internal transfer processes.

On 27 May 2025, at the call of SATSE, dozens of nurses protested outside the Basurto Hospital in Bilbao (Pais Vasco) to oppose the digital hiring system of the Basque health system, citing errors in the staffing lists, lack of interim contract calls and the suspension of internal transfer processes.

On 3 June 2025, in the morning, at the call of SATSE, nurses protested outside the hospital in Zumarraga (Pais Vasco) to oppose the digital hiring system of the Basque health system, citing errors in the staffing lists, lack of interim contract calls and the suspension of internal transfer processes.

On 13 June 2025, at the call of OGBL and LCGB, around 100 union members and supporters held a solidarity picket at the headquarters of Caritas Jeunes & Familles (CJF) in Howald (Luxembourg). The protest denounced the suspension of four OGBL union delegates, who had been placed on leave since October 2024, arguing that the disciplinary measures were retaliatory and linked to their union activities, emphasizing the precarious situation of the delegates, some of whom were left without salary, and demanded their reinstatement and stronger legal protections for union representatives.

On 14 June 2025, in the afternoon, at the call of OGBL and LCGB, around 100 people gathered in Howald (Luxembourg) to denounce the suspension of four union delegates from Caritas Jeunes & Familles and to call for stronger legal protection of workplace representatives.

On 21 June 2025, dozens of residents and representatives of more than 50 local associations gathered in Napoli (Campania) to protest the suspension, from 23 June to 15 September 2025, of three stops on Line 1 of the subway for maintenance work.

On 1 July 2025, around midday, at the call of the student initiative Perpetrator-Free HU (HU Taterfrei), around 50 students staged a protest outside Humboldt University in Berlin - Mitte (Berlin) against the reinstatement of a professor accused of sexualized violence and abuse of power, demanding greater transparency and protection for affected individuals and condemning the university's decision to allow the professor to resume teaching despite an ongoing disciplinary process, calling for his suspension, an independent investigation, and stronger support structures for victims.

On 4 July 2025, around 350 people, including health workers and FO union members, gathered outside the hospital in Laval (Pays de la Loire) to protest during the visit of the Minister of Health, Yannick Neuder, who confirmed the freeze on job cuts and the suspension of the investment attractiveness and sustainability plan. Protesters demanded written guarantees that no alternative austerity plan would be introduced.

On 12 July 2025, at least 150 people protested in Adeje (Canarias) to demand the suspension of the Cuna del Alma tourist development, located between two protected areas and on land of geological and archaeological interest. Protesters denounced the project as a threat to the environment, local heritage, and quality of life, and accused the mayor (in office for over three decades) of fostering destructive and exclusionary urban policies. They also demanded his resignation, citing concerns over forced expropriations and unchecked tourism-driven development.

On 15 July 2025, protesters gathered in Sofia in front of the 'Mothers' Home' hospital to support a woman who accused an obstetrician of assault during childbirth. They demanded Shikova's suspension and a full probe into obstetric violence. At the same time, , medical workers and patients gathered to support the doctor and defend her reputation, insisting she be presumed innocent until medical authorities complete their inquiries.

On 16 July 2025, around 200 Serd employees gathered outside the headquarters of ASL 3 in Genova (Liguria) to demonstrate against ASL 3 and regional health-authority policies, most immediately the 'temporary' suspension (from 1 August 2025) of the only Saturday and holiday methadone distribution clinic in Genoa due to staff shortages and, more broadly, the systematic under-resourcing of Serd and counselling services, operating at just 75 percent of required staffing, with no meaningful dialogue or hiring plan.
On 17 July 2025, sailors demonstrated off the shore of Akranes (coded to the coast of Akranes) to protest the suspension of coastal fishing and demand the resumption of their fishing quotas.
On 17 July 2025, sailors demonstrated off the shore of Dalvik (coded to the coast of Dalvik) to protest the suspension of coastal fishing and demand the resumption of their fishing quotas.
On 17 July 2025, sailors demonstrated off the shore of Djupivogur (coded to the coast of Djupivogur) to protest the suspension of coastal fishing and demand the resumption of their fishing quotas.

On 17 July 2025, sailors demonstrated off the shore of Grundarfjordur (coded to the coast of Grundarfjordur) to protest the suspension of coastal fishing and demand the resumption of their fishing quotas.
On 17 July 2025, sailors demonstrated off the shore of Olafsvik (coded to the coast of Olafsvik) to protest the suspension of coastal fishing and demand the resumption of their fishing quotas.

On 17 July 2025, sailors demonstrated off the shore of Patreksfjordur (coded to the coast of Patreksfjordur) to protest the suspension of coastal fishing and demand the resumption of their fishing quotas.
On 17 July 2025, sailors demonstrated off the shore of Sandgerdi (coded to the coast of Sandgerdi) to protest the suspension of coastal fishing and demand the resumption of their fishing quotas.

On 10 August 2025, in the afternoon, around 400 people gathered at the Vught Penitentiary in Vught (Noord-Brabant) to demand the release of an imprisoned lawyer and anti-coronavirus regulation protest leader. The protesters dressed predominantly in white, carried drums and banners, and formed a 'Ring of Connection' to show solidarity with the lawyer and his family. The lawyer was arrested on 11 June for alleged involvement in a criminal network potentially planning an attack ahead of the NATO summit in Brussels, leading to his suspension and removal from the bar. The protesters claimed his detention was politically motivated for the lawyer's stance against the Dutch government.

On 14 August 2025, members of the Road Transport Employers' Association (APOTA) held a peaceful protest for the third time in the past months outside the National Road Transport Agency (ANTA) office in Chisinau - Centru. Demonstrators demanded approval of a tariff calculation methodology, withdrawal of requirements to adapt vehicles for persons with disabilities, and suspension of eGPS and e-ticketing projects pending consultations. ANTA responded that it was open to dialogue and clarified that the accessibility request was informational, not a restriction.

On 26 August 2025, around 40 CGT and SUD affiliates from across Normandy gathered outside La Poste's regional headquarters in Rouen (Normandie) to protest disciplinary sanctions against a unionized postal worker. The worker, also a union delegate, was accused of allowing the president of a public services defense association to enter the site in April 2025 while delivering a petition against post office closures. Unions denounced the sanction procedure, which could lead to suspension, and called for its abandonment.

On 29 August 2025, around 200 people marched in Adeje (Canarias) to demand the suspension of the Cuna del Alma tourist development, located between two protected areas and on land of geological and archaeological interest. Protesters denounced the project's environmental and heritage impact and called for its immediate halt and accountability from both the developer and the Adeje municipality.
